findings: The draining-pool hook gives students exactly one point and one rate, which matches the lesson objective before formulas appear.
The overview clearly distinguishes when each form is useful instead of presenting three forms as unrelated memorization.
Both pause problems are solvable from prior slides and are followed by answer-reveal slides with intermediate algebra, not just final equations.
The inner-minus trap is called out explicitly with a wrong/right comparison, targeting the common y - (-2) sign error.
The standard-form slide calls out clearing every term and keeping A non-negative, which addresses two common conversion mistakes.
The closing path tells students what to do next in the Learn Portal: read, practice, complete the assignment, and use advisor check-in.
watch_points: The standard-form conversion slide contains several algebra steps in one frame; it is readable in the contact sheet, but students may benefit from pausing there.
Students who confuse point-slope form with slope-intercept form may need extra practice before the assignment.
